posts.title: Romantic Junk Journal Ideas for a Quiet Evening
posts.excerpt: A soft, saveable list of romantic junk journal ideas for nights when you want lace, candlelight, old paper, and one small memory worth keeping.

posts.title: How to Build a Junk Journal Corner You Never Want to Leave
posts.excerpt: A window, a tray of treasures, one honest lamp — how to turn any spare corner into the place your hobby lives, and why it changes how often you create.

posts.title: 50 Things You Should Never Throw Away If You Junk Journal
posts.excerpt: The tickets, tags, teabag papers and torn envelopes hiding in your house right now — a keep-list to pin above your bin, plus what to do with all of it.

posts.title: For Cat-Loving Journal Keepers: A Soft Floral Whisker Theme
posts.excerpt: Watercolor cats tucked into rose baskets and lavender — an aesthetic for the gentlest kind of journal, with its real palette and ways to use it.

posts.title: A Vintage Dog Show in Your Junk Journal: Ideas for Dog Lovers
posts.excerpt: Prize rosettes, ribbon medals, and solemn little champions — how to build a warm, old-fashioned dog-show spread, plus the palette that holds it together.

posts.title: A Wedding Junk Journal Theme in Silver, Cream, and Candlelight
posts.excerpt: Inside the Silver Vow theme — pewter-leafed peonies, pearls, and church light — and who this quiet wedding palette is really for.

posts.title: Colorful Junk Journal Ideas for Maximalists (and a Palette to Steal)
posts.excerpt: When soft vintage neutrals feel too quiet — a color-drunk theme, a six-shade palette pulled from real pages, and seven ways to use it in your next spread.
